# Task 19 documentation and CI configuration (2026-03-13 06:35Z)

_**User**_

Implement Task 19 from docs/plans/2026-03-13-dev-shell-impl-plan.md.

**Task 19: 跨平台与文档**

1. Create or update README.md in project root with:
   - Project name and one-line description: development shell with virtual FS, cross-platform (Linux, macOS, Windows), written in Rust.
   - Features: virtual filesystem, .bin persistence, built-in commands only (no host process execution), export-readonly to host temp dir.
   - Build: cargo build. Run: cargo run [path] (path optional, .bin file).
   - List built-in commands: pwd, cd, ls, mkdir, cat, touch, echo, save, export-readonly, exit/quit.
   - Short usage example: start shell, pwd, mkdir /foo, save test.bin, exit; then cargo run -- test.bin, ls /.
   - Optional: export-readonly example (run command, paste path from stdout to inspect files on host).

2. Optionally add .github/workflows/ci.yml: one job, matrix strategy with os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest], steps: checkout, install Rust (actions-rs or rust-toolchain), cargo test.

3. Run cargo test to confirm nothing broken. Commit: git add README.md (and .github/workflows/ci.yml if created) && git commit -m "docs: README and usage"

Work directory: /home/ars/richard/2026/pvm_2/dev_shell
Report: what was written, commit.

On GitHub Actions, the simplest is to use the official actions/setup-rust or the built-in Rust support. I'll use actions/checkout and a standard rust setup - typically people use dtolnay/rust-toolchain or actions-rs/toolchain. - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable

Then run cargo test. I'll keep it minimal: checkout, rust-toolchain stable, cargo test.
